title: in membership operator
manager: An `in` operator for membership testing against sets, ranges, and containers — `x in #[a b c]`, `n in 0..9` — so the single most common guard shape reads in one glance instead of a chain of ORs. Small on its own, but it is the difference between a guard a reviewer can read at a glance and one they have to mentally parse into a truth table.
cs: `in` over sets/bags/ranges/containers; total; desugars to the container membership protocol (W6.43).
